findings.title: Missing initializer leaves ownership and core token state unset, permanently bricking the token

best_agent_match.title: Missing initializer leaves owner and core token state unset, permanently bricking the token

findings.title: `_mainPool` is never assigned, so ordinary transfers and unsupported-recipient sells revert

best_agent_match.title: Main pool is never assigned, so ordinary transfers and unsupported sells revert

findings.title: Mint and burn logic is manipulable because it trusts raw instantaneous pair balances and public counter sync

best_agent_match.title: Buy-side supply expansion is flash-loan manipulable through instantaneous AMM balances

findings.title: Epoch rollover snapshots stale counters before refreshing pool state, allowing poisoned baselines for an entire epoch

best_agent_match.title: Epoch baselines can be poisoned because rollover snapshots stale cached counters before refreshing pool state

findings.title: Pool creation is fully sandwichable because swap and liquidity add both use zero slippage protection

best_agent_match.title: Pool creation is fully sandwichable because both swap and liquidity add use zero slippage bounds

findings.title: Updating the liquidity reserve burns and strands protocol funds because the migration transfer is taxed

best_agent_match.title: Reentrancy Vulnerability in setLiquidityReserve and setStabilizer

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy Vulnerability in setLiquidityReserve and setStabilizer
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. ERC20 balance moves in `_transfer()` do not invoke recipient callbacks, and the function makes no attacker-controlled external state-changing calls before updating storage.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by Zero in getFactor when _totalSupply becomes 0
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. This is a speculative terminal-state edge case without a concrete practical path in the reviewed source, and the contract is already bricked much earlier by missing initialization.

rejected_candidates.title: Arbitrary External Calls via Owner-Controlled Addresses
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. Setting reserve or stabilizer to a contract address does not by itself create arbitrary callback execution; token transfers only update internal accounting.

rejected_candidates.title: Taxless Setter Privilege Escalation Allows Fee Bypass
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. This is an intended owner-controlled privilege model, not a separate vulnerability absent compromise of a privileged role.

rejected_candidates.title: No Validation on Lock Box Unlock Time Bounds
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. The reviewed source contains no tranche-creation path, so there is no supported way to create a zero-unlock-time tranche from this code alone.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner Can Remap Lock Box Beneficiary After Vesting Period
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. `reassignTranche()` explicitly requires `unlockTime > now`, so reassignment after vesting is not allowed.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Bounds Check on Tranche Index in getLockBoxes
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. Solidity array access reverts on out-of-bounds indices; it does not return default values.

rejected_candidates.title: Silent Pool State Updates Enable Price Manipulation
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected as a standalone issue. The lack of an event is not the root bug; the real reportable problem is the manipulable balance-based oracle captured in F-003.

rejected_candidates.title: Out of Bounds Access in getSupportedPools
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. Solidity array indexing reverts on out-of-bounds access; it does not return `address(0)`.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited Minting During Presale Phase
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. The token does not itself enforce a presale cap, but presale authorization/cap logic may live in the external presale contract, and in this source presale minting is currently unreachable due to missing initialization.

rejected_candidates.title: Block Timestamp Dependency for Epoch Updates
rejected_candidates.reason: Rejected. This is ordinary timestamp-based epoch logic and does not rise to a realistic reportable issue on its own.
